title: ORAC single-crate, multi-binary
when_to_use: Single deployment target with shared library + multiple bin entry points. Use one Cargo crate, [[bin]] x N entries, lib lives in src/lib.rs. Avoids LCM workspace-of-crates overhead.
exemplar: src/lib.rs declares workflow_core; src/bin/wf-crystallise/main.rs + src/bin/wf-dispatch/main.rs are the two binaries; Cargo.toml has [lib] + two [[bin]] sections.

title: Cluster D early-ship (trust before substrate)
when_to_use: When a cross-cutting aspect layer (build-prereqs, namespace invariants, CI gates, lifecycle decay) governs all downstream readers. Ship the aspect layer Day 1 before authoring the first reader.
exemplar: Cluster D (m8 build-prereq + m9 namespace guard + m10 Ember CI gate + m11 lifecycle decay) authored before any Cluster A reader (m1/m2/m3).

title: cargo:rustc-cfg=povm_calibrated env-only gate
when_to_use: When a build-time invariant must NOT be bypassable via Cargo --features. Use a build.rs that emits cargo:rustc-cfg=foo based on env probe; this cannot be overridden by feature flag.
exemplar: m8 build script reads stcortex schema version; if mismatch, refuses to set cfg(stcortex_calibrated). Module code uses #[cfg(stcortex_calibrated)] guards.

title: plan.toml as machine-readable PASS/FAIL authority
when_to_use: When phase transitions must be driven by code-readable state (not prose). Top-of-file authoring block + per-phase status fields readable by scripts.
exemplar: plan.toml has [authorization] with g9_fired = false; gate.sh refuses to advance until g9_fired = true. Prose declarations in markdown have no authority.

title: Feature gate matrix (api/intelligence/monitoring/evolution)
when_to_use: When you want per-cluster opt-in without the complexity of workspace crates. Use Cargo features keyed to clusters; Cluster D is NEVER feature-gated (aspect-layer invariant).
exemplar: [features] in Cargo.toml: api = []; intelligence = []; monitoring = []; evolution = []; full = ['api','intelligence','monitoring','evolution']. m8/m9/m10/m11 have no #[cfg(feature=...)] gates.

title: workflow_core shared lib INSIDE same crate
when_to_use: When binaries share types/schemas/constants. Put the shared code in src/lib.rs of the same crate as the binaries; no workspace member needed. Reduces gate-script complexity.
exemplar: Cargo.toml: [lib] name = 'workflow_core'; [[bin]] name = 'wf-crystallise' + [[bin]] name = 'wf-dispatch'. Binaries do `use workflow_core::namespace::*;`

title: Layer docstring style (Layer/Deps/Tests/Features/Platform/Impl Notes/Related Docs)
when_to_use: Every module file. Lifted from ME v2 resources.rs convention.

title: thiserror error-enum taxonomy + ? propagation
when_to_use: Any fallible function. Every cluster A/C/D/G/H has a typed error enum; ? propagates through the call chain.
exemplar: #[derive(thiserror::Error, Debug)] pub enum IngestError { #[error('atuin db unavailable: {0}')] DbUnavailable(#[from] rusqlite::Error), #[error('namespace violation: {0}')] Namespace(String) }

title: tracing-subscriber + structured emit
when_to_use: All daemon/library output. No println!/eprintln! (SIGPIPE risk per ORAC BUG-018).
exemplar: tracing::info!(module_id = %m_id, lift = %lift_value, n = %sample_n, 'lift evidence emitted');

title: Counter / gauge / histogram + rolling window metrics
when_to_use: Cluster B/C/E/H. Per-module counters for emit volume, gauges for current cost, histograms for latency/lift distributions.
exemplar: metrics::counter!('wt_workflow_runs_total', 1, 'cluster' => 'C', 'kind' => 'observed'); metrics::histogram!('wt_lift_value', lift_value);

title: Signal-observation pattern (passive Phase A)
when_to_use: Cluster B/E/H. Observe substrate signals; do NOT mutate or dispatch from observer code.
exemplar: fn observe(signal: &Signal) -> ObservationRecord { ObservationRecord { ts, signal_kind: signal.kind(), payload: signal.snapshot() } }

title: Newtype discipline (SessionId, ConsumerId, ...)
when_to_use: Cluster A and any cross-module identifier. Wrap primitives to prevent mixing types at compile time.
exemplar: #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)] pub struct SessionId(String); impl SessionId { pub fn new(s: impl Into<String>) -> Self { Self(s.into()) } }

title: Central state-table + transitions
when_to_use: Cluster C/D/G. State machines with explicit transitions enumerated; refuse-on-invalid-transition rather than silent panic.
exemplar: match (current, event) { (Pending, Approved) => Active, (Active, Sunset) => Retired, (s, e) => return Err(StateError::InvalidTransition{from: s, on: e}) }

title: Feature flags + env override + TOML config
when_to_use: Cluster D (m8 build-script reads env + feature flags). Layered precedence: env > TOML > default.
exemplar: let cal = std::env::var('WT_STCORTEX_CALIBRATED').unwrap_or(toml_cfg.calibrated.to_string());

title: 12D tensor framing
when_to_use: Cluster B (m6 cost bands) + Cluster F (per-edge confidence). 12 named dimensions normalised to [0,1].
exemplar: pub struct Tensor12D { latency: f64, cost: f64, fitness: f64, density: f64, /* ... 8 more */ } - all in [0.0, 1.0].

title: Engine-knows-about-its-own-state (self-model)
when_to_use: Cluster G (m31 selection criteria). Engine exposes /self-model with state, elapsed, confidence, blockers.
exemplar: GET /self-model -> {state: 'selecting', elapsed_ms: 1234, candidates_considered: 17, blocked_by: []}

title: NAM substrate concepts (proposal-as-substrate-frame)
when_to_use: Cluster F. Use substrate-frame language ('observation', 'pathway', 'pressure') over command-frame ('execute', 'force', 'run').
exemplar: fn propose_variant(observed: &CompositionalPattern) -> ProposalRecord { /* substrate-frame: 'this is what was observed; here is a variant for review' */ }

title: 4-stage zero-tolerance quality gate
when_to_use: Before every commit. Order matters; each stage aborts on first failure.
exemplar: cargo check && cargo clippy -- -D warnings && cargo clippy -- -D warnings -W clippy::pedantic && cargo test --lib --release

title: PIPESTATUS[0] discipline in gate chains
when_to_use: Any gate chain using `cargo ... | tail`. tail's exit overrides cargo's; use ${PIPESTATUS[0]} to inspect cargo.
exemplar: cargo check 2>&1 | tail -20; [[ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} -ne 0 ]] && { echo 'check failed'; exit 2; }

title: bacon on_success chaining (check -> clippy -> pedantic)
when_to_use: Continuous feedback during development. on_success chains stages so you see pedantic warnings only when check + clippy are clean.
exemplar: bacon.toml: [jobs.check] on_success = 'clippy'; [jobs.clippy] on_success = 'pedantic'; export locations to .bacon-locations for nvim integration.

title: 50+ tests per module minimum
when_to_use: Every module. KEYSTONE Cluster F gets 250+. Forces design questions that surface architecture issues.
exemplar: #[cfg(test)] mod tests { /* 50+ #[test] functions covering happy + error + edge */ }

title: Lazy cursor-based pagination (atuin SQLite)
when_to_use: m1 reading 263k+ row atuin.db. Never SELECT * without LIMIT; use cursor (rowid > last) pagination.
exemplar: PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000; PRAGMA query_only = ON; SELECT rowid, command, timestamp FROM history WHERE rowid > ? ORDER BY rowid ASC LIMIT 500;

title: Subprocess CLI fallback
when_to_use: When SQLite path is unavailable. Wrap atuin CLI in tokio::process::Command with 5s timeout.
exemplar: tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), Command::new('atuin').args(['search', '--format=json']).output()).await

title: Reducer-callback dedup (stcortex consumer)
when_to_use: m2 narrows scope to tool_call + consumption events. Dedup at consumer (not at writer) using (session_id, event_id) key.
exemplar: let key = (event.session_id.clone(), event.event_id); if !seen.insert(key) { return Ok(()); }

title: JSONL one-event-per-file (pressure register)
when_to_use: m15 pressure register. Append-only, atomic emit, no shared lock.
exemplar: use std::os::unix::fs::OpenOptionsExt; OpenOptions::new().append(true).create(true).custom_flags(libc::O_APPEND).open(path)?.write_all(line.as_bytes())?;

title: JSONB consumer_inputs column (m7 hub)
when_to_use: m7 workflow_runs hub. CC-1 contract uses JOIN on hub.consumer_inputs JSONB column rather than sharing internal structs.
exemplar: CREATE TABLE workflow_runs (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, consumer_inputs JSONB NOT NULL, ...); -- query: SELECT * FROM workflow_runs WHERE consumer_inputs->>'pane_id' = ?

title: Wilson CI for lift / confidence (n<20 returns None)
when_to_use: m14 lift metric + m22 confidence. Avoids point-estimate over-claim; returns None for n<20 (insufficient evidence).
exemplar: fn wilson_ci(successes: u32, n: u32, z: f64) -> Option<(f64, f64)> { if n < 20 { return None; } /* Wilson formula */ }

title: EMA over Converged-excluded window (m6 context cost)
when_to_use: m6 20-session EMA. Explicitly excludes Converged outcomes to avoid feedback bias from successful workflows skewing cost baseline.
exemplar: let samples: Vec<_> = sessions.iter().filter(|s| s.outcome != Outcome::Converged).take(20).collect(); ema(samples, alpha=0.2)

title: Top-K-by-distance N=3 (m23 proposer)
when_to_use: m23 workflow proposer. Normalised Levenshtein distance: <0.25 same / 0.25-0.60 near-miss / >0.60 distinct. Returns top 3 distinct.
exemplar: candidates.sort_by(|a,b| dist(a, target).partial_cmp(&dist(b, target)).unwrap()); candidates.into_iter().filter(|c| dist(c, target) > 0.60).take(3).collect()

title: 3-band LTP/LTD gate (m13 stcortex writer)
when_to_use: m13 stcortex writer. substrate_LTP_density < 0.15 -> deferred JSONL buffer (backpressure); 0.15-0.50 -> write at half rate; >0.50 -> write at full rate.
exemplar: match substrate_LTP_density { d if d < 0.15 => buffer_jsonl(event), d if d < 0.50 => maybe_write_half(event), _ => write_full(event) }

title: EscapeSurfaceProfile ordinal enum
when_to_use: m30 bank + m32 dispatch + m9 namespace guard. Unified destructiveness schema; m32 displays before each step; m9 enforces at namespace level.
exemplar: #[repr(u8)] pub enum EscapeSurfaceProfile { Read = 0, IdempotentWrite = 1, Mutate = 2, Destructive = 3, Catastrophic = 4 } - banner_line() returns 'READ' / 'WRITE' / 'MUTATE' / 'DESTRUCTIVE!' / 'CATASTROPHIC!'

title: NexusEvent typed envelope (Cluster H)
when_to_use: m40 -> SYNTHEX v2 :8092 /v3/nexus/push. Typed schema; MVP per Cluster H spec uses Option A untyped JSON, M1+ tightens.
exemplar: POST :8092/v3/nexus/push -d '{"kind":"workflow.observed","source":"wf-crystallise","payload":{...}}'

title: LCM RPC lcm.loop.create (NOT lcm.deploy)
when_to_use: m41 -> LCM MCP stdio. Specifically: lcm.loop.create is correct; lcm.deploy is hypothetical/wrong (called out in vault).
exemplar: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"lcm.loop.create","params":{"workflow_id":"...","steps":[...]}}

title: stcortex pathway emit (Hebbian fitness_delta)
when_to_use: m42 -> stcortex pathway write. fitness_delta: PassVerified=+0.25, Pass=+0.15, Blocked=-0.05, Fail=-0.10. Namespace workflow_trace_*.
exemplar: stcortex call write_pathway 'workflow_<id>_observed' 'workflow_<id>_verified' 'workflow_trace_pathways' 0.25 '<session>' null

title: Outbox-first JSONL buffer (Cluster H resilience)
when_to_use: m40/m41/m42. Write to outbox file first, then RPC. Survives substrate outage; replay drains outbox once RPC recovers.
exemplar: append_jsonl('~/.local/share/workflow-trace/outbox.jsonl', event); spawn(rpc_emit_then_clear(event.id))

title: Circuit breaker on 2 consecutive peer failures
when_to_use: m40/m41/m42. After 2 consecutive failures, open breaker; fall back to outbox-only; log Watcher Class-I notice.
exemplar: if consecutive_fail >= 2 { breaker.open(); watcher_notify('Class-I', format!('peer {} unhealthy', peer)); }
